channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Rainbow Six Siege Stage 1 Schedules—Esports World Cup Qualifiers, Explained
item.content:encoded:
Ubisoft has announced that the Rainbow Six Siege Stage 1 circuit starts very soon, and that it’s the opener of the qualification path to the fast-approaching Esports World Cup. Following a drastic change, the Esports World Cup will now take place in Paris instead of Riyadh, which means millions more fans are more likely to attend, eager to see all the action, including Rainbow Six esports.

From the Asia Pacific League to the CNL, you’ll find every Rainbow Six Stage 1 schedule below, including how teams qualify for the Esports World Cup and what the prize pools are worth.

Quick Navigation

  • North America League
  • South America League
  • Europe MENA League
  • China League
  • How Does Stage 1 Set Up for the Esports World Cup?
  • How to Watch Rainbow Six Siege Esports

Be warned – this is a long one! But every schedule, team list, and prize pool is outlined below for Stage 1 of the Rainbow Six Siege esports circuit.

Asia Pacific League

Everything in the opening stage of the Rainbow Six Siege esports schedule towards the EWC starts with the APAC League. Here’s the full Oceania roster for this season:

  • Enterprise
  • Shaiikademy
  • Chiefs EC
  • Team Anios
  • Man Esports LFO
  • Barbie Boys
  • Rock LFO
  • 6Targets

Here’s the R6 APAC League Stage schedule for the APL Oceania:

Round 1: June 8th

  • Match 1 (18:00 AEST) – Shaiikademy vs Barbie Boys
  • Match 2 (19:00 AEST) – Rock LFO vs Man LFO
  • Match 3 (20:00 AEST) – 6Targets vs ENTERPRISE
  • Match 4 (21:00 AEST) – Team Anios vs Chiefs ESC

Round 2: June 9th

  • Match 1 (18:00 AEST) – Man LFO vs Shaiikademy
  • Match 2 (19:00 AEST) – Team Anios vs ENTERPRISE
  • Match 3 (20:00 AEST) – Barbie Boys vs Chiefs ESC
  • Match 4 (21:00 AEST) – Rock LFO vs 6Targets

Round 3: June 15th

  • Match 1 (18:00 AEST) – Man LFO vs Barbie Boys
  • Match 2 (19:00 AEST) – Shaiikademy vs ENTERPRISE
  • Match 3 (20:00 AEST) – Rock LFO vs Team Anios
  • Match 4 (21:00 AEST) – 6Targets vs Chiefs ESC

Round 4: June 16th

  • Match 1 (18:00 AEST) – Team Anios vs Man LFO
  • Match 2 (19:00 AEST) – Barbie Boys vs 6Targets
  • Match 3 (20:00 AEST) – Chiefs ESC vs Shaiikademy
  • Match 4 (21:00 AEST) – ENTERPRISE vs Rock LFO

Round 5: June 22nd

  • Match 1 (18:00 AEST) – Man LFO vs 6Targets
  • Match 2 (19:00 AEST) – Team Anios vs Shaiikademy
  • Match 3 (20:00 AEST) – Barbie Boys vs Rock LFO
  • Match 4 (21:00 AEST) – Chiefs ESC vs ENTERPRISE

Round 6: June 23rd

  • Match 1 (18:00 AEST) – 6Targets vs Team Anios
  • Match 2 (19:00 AEST) – Chiefs ESC vs Man LFO
  • Match 3 (20:00 AEST) – ENTREPRISE vs Barbie Boys
  • Match 4 (21:00 AEST) – Shaiikademy vs Rock LFO

Round 7: June 29th

  • Match 1 (18:00 AEST) – Shaiikademy vs 6Targets
  • Match 2 (19:00 AEST) – Barbie Boys vs Team Anios
  • Match 3 (20:00 AEST) – Rock LFO vs Chiefs ESC
  • Match 4 (21:00 AEST) – Man LFO vs ENTERPRISE

And here’s the roster breakdown for the APL Asia:

  • Elevate
  • Soul’s Heart Esport
  • Weibo Gaming
  • Team Orchid
  • Daystar
  • Unreal Nightmare
  • Fury
  • HL Tauri

If you’re interested in watching the APL Asia, here’s the Stage 1 schedule:

Round 1: June 10th

  • Match 1 (18:00 SGT) – Team Orchid vs HL Tauri Esport
  • Match 2 (19:00 SGT) – Soul’s Heart vs FURY Global
  • Match 3 (20:00 SGT) – Unreal Nightmare vs Weibo Gaming
  • Match 4 (21:00 SGT) – DAYSTAR vs Elevate

Round 2: June 11th

  • Match 1 (18:00 SGT) – FURY Global vs Unreal Nightmare
  • Match 2 (19:00 SGT) – DAYSTAR vs Team Orchid
  • Match 3 (20:00 SGT) – HL Tauri Esport vs Soul’s Heart
  • Match 4 (21:00 SGT) – Elevate vs Weibo Gaming

Round 3: June 17th

  • Match 1 (18:00 SGT) – FURY Global vs HL Tauri Esport
  • Match 2 (19:00 SGT) – Team Orchid vs Weibo Gaming
  • Match 3 (20:00 SGT) – Unreal Nightmare vs Elevate
  • Match 4 (21:00 SGT) – Soul’s Heart vs DAYSTAR

Round 4: June 18th

  • Match 1 (18:00 SGT) – DAYSTAR vs FURY Global
  • Match 2 (19:00 SGT) – Weibo Gaming vs Soul’s Heart
  • Match 3 (20:00 SGT) – HL Tauri Esport vs Unreal Nightmare
  • Match 4 (21:00 SGT) – Elevate vs Team Orchid

Round 5: June 24th

  • Match 1 (18:00 SGT) – HL Tauri Esport vs DAYSTAR
  • Match 2 (19:00 SGT) – Soul’s Heart vs Elevate
  • Match 3 (20:00 SGT) – Team Orchid vs Unreal Nightmare
  • Match 4 (21:00 SGT) – FURY Global vs Weibo Gaming

Round 6: June 25th

  • Match 1 (18:00 SGT) – Unreal Nightmare vs DAYSTAR
  • Match 2 (19:00 SGT) – Team Orchid vs Soul’s Heart
  • Match 3 (20:00 SGT) – Elevate vs FURY Global
  • Match 4 (21:00 SGT) – Weibo Gaming vs HL Tauri Esport

Round 7: June 30th

  • Match 1 (18:00 SGT) – FURY Global vs Team Orchid
  • Match 2 (19:00 SGT) – HL Tauri Esport vs Elevate
  • Match 3 (20:00 SGT) – Soul’s Heart vs Unreal Nightmare
  • Match 4 (21:00 SGT) – DAYSTAR vs Weibo Gaming

And finally, the APL North roster:

  • SCARZ
  • DPLUS KIA
  • CAG Osaka
  • Kinotrope GAming
  • BNK FearX
  • LFO No Name
  • Trippy Main Telecom
  • Can You Be My Enemy?

Here’s their schedule for Stage 1:

Round 1: June 12th

  • Match 1 (18:00 JST) – Trippy Main Telecom vs Can You Be My Enemy?
  • Match 2 (19:00 JST) – BNK FearX vs LFO No Name
  • Match 3 (20:00 JST) – SCARZ vs Kinotrope Gaming
  • Match 4 (21:00 JST) – DPlus Kia vs CAG Osaka

Round 2: June 13th

  • Match 1 (18:00 JST) – Can You Be My Enemy? vs SCARZ
  • Match 2 (19:00 JST) – CAG OSAKA vs LFO No Name
  • Match 3 (20:00 JST) – Kinotrope Gaming vs BNK FearX
  • Match 4 (21:00 JST) – DPlus Kia vs Trippy Main Telecom

Round 3: June 19th

  • Match 1 (18:00 JST) – Trippy Main Telecom vs LFO No Name
  • Match 2 (19:00 JST) – Kinotrope Gaming vs Can You Be My Enemy?
  • Match 3 (20:00 JST) – SCARZ vs DPlus Kia
  • Match 4 (21:00 JST) – BNK FearX vs CAG Osaka

Round 4: June 20th

  • Match 1 (18:00 JST) – LFO No Name vs SCARZ
  • Match 2 (19:00 JST) – CAG Osaka vs Trippy Main Telecom
  • Match 3 (20:00 JST) – Can You Be My Enemy? vs BNK FearX
  • Match 4 (21:00 JST) – DPlus Kia vs Kinotrope Gaming

Round 5: June 26th

  • Match 1 (18:00 JST) – Can You Be My Enemy? vs DPlus Kia
  • Match 2 (19:00 JST) – Trippy Main Telecom vs BNK FearX
  • Match 3 (20:00 JST) – Kinotrope Gaming vs LFO No Name
  • Match 4 (21:00 JST) – SCARZ vs CAG Osaka

Round 6: June 27th

  • Match 1 (18:00 JST) – Can You Be My Enemy? vs CAG Osaka
  • Match 2 (19:00 JST) – Kinotrope Gaming vs Trippy Main Telecom
  • Match 3 (20:00 JST) – SCARZ vs BNK FearX
  • Match 4 (21:00 JST) – DPlus Kia vs LFO No Name

Round 7: July 1st

  • Match 1 (18:00 JST) – LFO No Name vs Can You Be My Enemy?
  • Match 2 (19:00 JST) – Trippy Main Telecom vs SCARZ
  • Match 3 (20:00 JST) – CAG Osaka vs Kinotrope Gaming
  • Match 4 (21:00 JST) – BNK FearX vs DPlus Kia

APL Prize Pool Distribution:

  • 1st : $15,000.00 / 420 SI points
  • 2nd : $11,000.00 / 300 SI points
  • 3rd : $8,000.00 / 250 SI points
  • 4th : $7,000.00 / 200 SI points
  • 5th : $5,000.00 / 175 SI points
  • 6th : $4,000.00 / 140 SI points
  • 7th : $2,000.00 / 100 SI points
  • 8th : $2,000.00 / 70 SI points
  • 9th : $0 / 35 SI points

North America League

Here’s the spread of teams competing in the NAL Stage 1 in the Rainbow Six Siege space:

  • Cloud9
  • Shopify Rebellion
  • M80
  • 100 Thieves
  • Spacestation
  • Five Fears
  • Wildcard
  • 1 of 30
  • DARKZERO
  • Outlast

And the round robin schedule for the first stage:

Round 1: June 10th

  • Match 1 (14:00 ET) – DarkZero Esports vs 1 of 30
  • Match 2 (15:00 ET) – M80 vs Spacestation Gaming
  • Match 3 (16:00 ET) – Wildcard vs 100 Thieves
  • Match 4 (17:00 ET) – Shopify Rebellion vs Cloud9
  • Match 5 (18:00 ET) – Five Fears vs Outlast

Round 2: June 11th

  • Match 1 (14:00 ET) – M80 vs 100 Thieves
  • Match 2 (15:00 ET) – Spacestation Gaming vs 1 of 30
  • Match 3 (16:00 ET) – Five Fears vs Shopify Rebellion
  • Match 4 (17:00 ET) – DarkZero Esports vs Outlast
  • Match 5 (18:00 ET) – Wildcard vs Cloud9

Round 3: June 12th

  • Match 1 (14:00 ET) – Cloud9 vs 100 Thieves
  • Match 2 (15:00 ET) – M80 vs 1 of 30
  • Match 3 (16:00 ET) – DarkZero Esports vs Five Fears
  • Match 4 (17:00 ET) – Spacestation Gaming vs Outlast
  • Match 5 (18:00 ET) – Wildcard vs Shopify Rebellion

Round 4: June 18th

  • Match 1 (14:00 ET) – Spacestation Gaming vs Five Fears
  • Match 2 (15:00 ET) – 1 of 30 vs Outlast
  • Match 3 (16:00 ET) – 100 Thieves vs Shopify Rebellion
  • Match 4 (17:00 ET) – M80 vs Cloud9
  • Match 5 (18:00 ET) – DarkZero Esports vs Wildcard

Round 5: June 19th

  • Match 1 (14:00 ET) – Shopify Rebellion vs 1 of 30
  • Match 2 (15:00 ET) – M80 vs Outlast
  • Match 3 (16:00 ET) – 100 Thieves vs DarkZero Esports
  • Match 4 (17:00 ET) – Cloud9 vs Five Fears
  • Match 5 (18:00 ET) – Spacestation Gaming vs Wildcard

Round 6: June 25th

  • Match 1 (14:00 ET) – Outlast vs Cloud9
  • Match 2 (15:00 ET) – 100 Thieves vs Five Fears
  • Match 3 (16:00 ET) – M80 vs Shopify Rebellion
  • Match 4 (17:00 ET) – 1 of 30 vs Wildcard
  • Match 5 (18:00 ET) – Spacestation Gaming vs DarkZero Esports

Round 7: June 26th

  • Match 1 (14:00 ET) – M80 vs Five Fears
  • Match 2 (15:00 ET) – Wildcard vs Outcast
  • Match 3 (16:00 ET) – Shopify Rebellion vs DarkZero Esports
  • Match 4 (17:00 ET) – Cloud9 vs Spacestation Gaming
  • Match 5 (18:00 ET) – 100 Thieves vs 1 of 30

Round 8: July 2nd

  • Match 1 (14:00 ET) – Five Fears vs Wildcard
  • Match 2 (15:00 ET) – M80 vs DarkZero Esports
  • Match 3 (16:00 ET) – Shopify Rebellion vs SpaceStation Gaming
  • Match 4 (17:00 ET) – Outlast vs 100 Thieves
  • Match 5 (18:00 ET) – Cloud9 vs 1 of 30

Round 9: July 3rd

  • Match 1 (14:00 ET) – M80 vs Wildcard
  • Match 2 (15:00 ET) – DarkZero Esports vs Cloud9
  • Match 3 (16:00 ET) – 100 Thieves vs SpaceStation Gaming
  • Match 4 (17:00 ET) – Shopify Rebellion vs 100 Thieves
  • Match 5 (18:00 ET) – 1 of 30 vs Five Fears

NAL Prize Pool Distribution:

  • 1st : $25,000.00 / 420 SI points
  • 2nd : $22,000.00 / 300 SI points
  • 3rd : $19,000.00 / 250 SI points
  • 4th : $16,000.00 / 200 SI points
  • 5th : $13,000.00 / 175 SI points
  • 6th : $11,000.00 / 140 SI points
  • 7th : $10,000.00 / 100 SI points
  • 8th : $8,000.00 / 70 SI points
  • 9th : $8,000.00 / 35 SI points
  • 10th : $8,000.00 / 0 SI points

South America League

Here are the teams competing in the South America League in Stage 1:

  • FURIA
  • Ninjas in Pyjamas
  • Fluxo W7M
  • Black Dragons
  • FaZe Clan
  • LOS
  • Team Liquid Alienware
  • Imperial Esports
  • LOUD
  • INTZ

And here’s the full list of fixtures in the Stage 1 schedule for the Rainbow Six Siege esports space:

Round 1: June 13th

  • Match 1 (13:00 BRT) – INTZ vs TL Alienware
  • Match 2 (14:15 BRT) – Imperial Esports vs FaZe Clan
  • Match 3 (15:30 BRT) – Black Dragons vs Fluxo.W7M
  • Match 4 (16:45 BRT) – LOUD vs Ninjas in Pyjamas
  • Match 5 (18:00 BRT) – LOS vs Furia

Round 2: June 14th

  • Match 1 (13:00 BRT) – Imperial Esports vs TL Alienware
  • Match 2 (14:15 BRT) – INTZ vs Furia
  • Match 3 (15:30 BRT) – Black Dragons vs Ninjas in Pyjamas
  • Match 4 (16:45 BRT) – LOS vs LOUD
  • Match 5 (18:00 BRT) – FaZe Clan vs Fluxo W7M

Round 3: June 20th

  • Match 1 (13:00 BRT) – Imperial Esports vs Fluxo W7M
  • Match 2 (14:15 BRT) – TL Alienware vs Furia
  • Match 3 (15:30 BRT) – INTZ vs LOUD
  • Match 4 (16:45 BRT) – Black Dragons vs LOS
  • Match 5 (18:00 BRT) – FaZe Clan vs Ninjas in Pyjamas

Round 4: June 21st

  • Match 1 (13:00 BRT) – TL Alienware vs LOUD
  • Match 2 (14:15 BRT) – Imperial Esports vs Furia
  • Match 3 (15:30 BRT) – Fluxo W7M vs Ninjas in Pyjamas
  • Match 4 (16:45 BRT) – FaZe Clan vs LOS
  • Match 5 (18:00 BRT) – INTZ vs Black Dragons

Round 5: June 27th

  • Match 1 (13:00 BRT) – Furia vs LOUD
  • Match 2 (14:15 BRT) – Fluxo W7M vs LOS
  • Match 3 (15:30 BRT) – Imperial Esports vs Ninjas in Pyjamas
  • Match 4 (16:45 BRT) – TL Alienware vs Black Dragons
  • Match 5 (18:00 BRT) – FaZe Clan vs INTZ

Round 6: June 28th

  • Match 1 (13:00 BRT) – Imperial Esports vs LOUD
  • Match 2 (14:15 BRT) – Ninjas in Pyjamas vs LOS
  • Match 3 (15:30 BRT) – Furia vs Black Dragons
  • Match 4 (16:45 BRT) – Fluxo W7M vs INTZ
  • Match 5 (18:00 BRT) – TL Alienware vs FaZe Clan

Round 7: July 4th

  • Match 1 (13:00 BRT) – LOUD vs Black Dragons
  • Match 2 (14:15 BRT) – Imperial Esports vs LOS
  • Match 3 (15:30 BRT) – Ninjas in Pyjamas vs INTZ
  • Match 4 (16:45 BRT) – Furia vs FaZe Clan
  • Match 5 (18:00 BRT) – Fluxo W7M vs TL Alienware

Round 8: July 5th

  • Match 1 (13:00 BRT) – LOS vs INTZ
  • Match 2 (14:15 BRT) – LOUD vs FaZe Clan
  • Match 3 (15:30 BRT) – Imperial Esports vs Black Dragons
  • Match 4 (16:45 BRT) – Furia vs Fluxo W7M
  • Match 5 (18:00 BRT) – Ninjas in Pyjamas vs TL Alienware

Round 9: July 12th

  • Match 1 (13:00 BRT) – Imperial Esports vs INTZ
  • Match 2 (14:15 BRT) – Black Dragons vs FaZe Clan
  • Match 3 (15:30 BRT) – LOS vs TL Alienware
  • Match 4 (16:45 BRT) – LOUD vs Fluxo W7M
  • Match 5 (18:00 BRT) – Ninjas in Pyjamas vs Furia

SAL Prize Pool Distribution:

  • 1st : $25,000.00 / 420 SI points
  • 2nd : $22,000.00 / 300 SI points
  • 3rd : $19,000.00 / 250 SI points
  • 4th : $16,000.00 / 200 SI points
  • 5th : $13,000.00 / 175 SI points
  • 6th : $11,000.00 / 140 SI points
  • 7th : $10,000.00 / 100 SI points
  • 8th : $8,000.00 / 70 SI points
  • 9th : $8,000.00 / 35 SI points
  • 10th : $8,000.00 / 0 SI points

Europe MENA League

Here are the top-tier teams playing in the EML this season:

  • G2 Esports
  • Virtus.pro
  • Team Falcons
  • Twisted Minds
  • Shifters
  • Geekay Esports
  • Team Secret
  • Team Heretics
  • Fnatic
  • Rebels Gaming

And their Stage 1 schedule:

Round 1: June 8th

  • Match 1 (18:00 CEST) – Virtus.Pro vs Fnatic
  • Match 2 (19:00 CEST) – Shifters vs Twisted Minds
  • Match 3 (20:00 CEST) – G2 Esports vs Geekay Esports
  • Match 4 (21:00 CEST) – Team Falcons vs RBLS Gaming
  • Match 5 (22:00 CEST) – Team Secret vs Team Heretics

Round 2: June 9th

  • Match 1 (18:00 CEST) – Fnatic vs Geekay Esports
  • Match 2 (19:00 CEST) – Virtus.Pro vs RBLS Gaming
  • Match 3 (20:00 CEST) – Team Falcons vs Twisted Minds
  • Match 4 (21:00 CEST) – G2 Esports vs Team Heretics
  • Match 5 (22:00 CEST) – Shifters vs Team Secret

Round 3: June 15th

  • Match 1 (18:00 CEST) – RBLS Gaming vs Twisted Minds
  • Match 2 (19:00 CEST) – Virtus.Pro vs Geekay Esports
  • Match 3 (20:00 CEST) – G2 Esports vs Shifters
  • Match 4 (21:00 CEST) – Team Falcons vs Team Secret
  • Match 5 (22:00 CEST) – Fnatic vs Team Heretics

Round 4: June 16th

  • Match 1 (18:00 CEST) – Virtus.Pro vs Twisted Minds
  • Match 2 (19:00 CEST) – RBLS Gaming vs Team Secret
  • Match 3 (20:00 CEST) – Team Falcons vs G2 Esports
  • Match 4 (21:00 CEST) – Fnatic vs Shifters
  • Match 5 (22:00 CEST) – Geekay Esports vs Team Heretics

Round 5: June 22nd

  • Match 1 (18:00 CEST) – Fnatic vs Team Falcons
  • Match 2 (19:00 CEST) – Geekay Esports vs Shifters
  • Match 3 (20:00 CEST) – RBLS Gaming vs G2 Esports
  • Match 4 (21:00 CEST) – Twisted Minds vs Team Secret
  • Match 5 (22:00 CEST) – Virtus.Pro vs Team Heretics

Round 6: June 23rd

  • Match 1 (18:00 CEST) – RBLS Gaming vs Fnatic
  • Match 2 (19:00 CEST) – Virtus.Pro vs Team Secret
  • Match 3 (20:00 CEST) – Twisted Minds vs G2 Esports
  • Match 4 (21:00 CEST) – Geekay Esports vs Team Falcons
  • Match 5 (22:00 CEST) – Team Heretics vs Shifters

Round 7: June 24th

  • Match 1 (18:00 CEST) – Geekay Esports vs RBLS Gaming
  • Match 2 (19:00 CEST) – Team Heretics vs Team Falcons
  • Match 3 (20:00 CEST) – Team Secret vs G2 Esports
  • Match 4 (21:00 CEST) – Twisted Minds vs Fnatic
  • Match 5 (22:00 CEST) – Virtus.Pro vs Shifters

Round 8: June 29th

  • Match 1 (18:00 CEST) – Team Secret vs Fnatic
  • Match 2 (19:00 CEST) – Twisted Minds vs Geekay Esports
  • Match 3 (20:00 CEST) – Virtus.Pro vs G2 Esports
  • Match 4 (21:00 CEST) – Shifters vs Team Falcons
  • Match 5 (22:00 CEST) – Team Heretics vs RBLS Gaming

Round 9: June 30th

  • Match 1 (18:00 CEST) – Team Secret vs Geekay Esports
  • Match 2 (19:00 CEST) – Shifters vs RBLS Gaming
  • Match 3 (20:00 CEST) – Virtus.Pro vs Team Falcons
  • Match 4 (21:00 CEST) – G2 Esports vs Fnatic
  • Match 5 (22:00 CEST) – Team Heretics vs Twisted Minds

EML Prize Pool Distribution:

  • 1st : $25,000.00 / 420 SI points
  • 2nd : $22,000.00 / 300 SI points
  • 3rd : $19,000.00 / 250 SI points
  • 4th : $16,000.00 / 200 SI points
  • 5th : $13,000.00 / 175 SI points
  • 6th : $11,000.00 / 140 SI points
  • 7th : $10,000.00 / 100 SI points
  • 8th : $8,000.00 / 70 SI points
  • 9th : $8,000.00 / 35 SI points
  • 10th : $8,000.00 / 0 SI points

China League

The CNL features the talents of ten teams in Stage 1:

  • Four Angry Men
  • EDward Gaming
  • Leviatan Soul
  • One Coin
  • Titan Esports Club
  • TYLOO
  • Pozy
  • MyQueen
  • KingZero Esports
  • All Gamers

Here’s their schedule going into Stage 1 of the Esports World Cup qualifying path:

Round 1: June 5th

  • Match 1 (13:30 CST) – All Gamers vs My Queen
  • Match 2 (17:00 CST) – EDward Gaming vs Titan Esports Club
  • Match 3 (20:30 CST) – KingZero vs Pozy

Round 2: June 6th

  • Match 1 (13:30 CST) – Leviatan Soul vs One Coin
  • Match 2 (17:00 CST) – 4 AM vs Tyloo
  • Match 3 (20:30 CST) – All Gamers vs Titan Esports Club

Round 3: June 7th

  • Match 1 (13:30 CST) – Tyloo vs KingZero
  • Match 2 (17:00 CST) – EDward Gaming vs One Coin
  • Match 3 (20:30 CST) – My Queen vs Pozy

Round 4: June 12th

  • Match 1 (13:30 CST) – Leviatan vs 4 AM
  • Match 2 (17:00 CST) – All Gamers vs Pozy
  • Match 3 (20:30 CST) – Titan Esports Club vs One Coin

Round 5: June 13th

  • Match 1 (13:30 CST) – My Queen vs Tyloo
  • Match 2 (17:00 CST) – EDward Gaming vs 4 AM
  • Match 3 (20:30 CST) – KingZero vs Leviatan

Round 6: June 14th

  • Match 1 (13:30 CST) – All Gamers vs One Coin
  • Match 2 (17:00 CST) – Pozy vs Tyloo
  • Match 3 (20:30 CST) – Titan Esports Club vs 4AM

Round 7: June 19th

  • Match 1 (13:30 CST) – My Queen vs Leviatan
  • Match 2 (17:00 CST) – EDward Gaming vs KingZero
  • Match 3 (20:30 CST) – All Gamers vs Tyloo

Round 8: June 20th

  • Match 1 (13:30 CST) – One Coin vs 4 AM
  • Match 2 (17:00 CST) – Pozy vs Leviatan
  • Match 3 (20:30 CST) – Titan Esports Club vs KingZero

Round 9: June 21st

  • Match 1 (13:30 CST) – My Queen vs EDward Gaming
  • Match 2 (17:00 CST) – All Gamers vs 4 AM
  • Match 3 (20:30 CST) – Tyloo vs Leviatan

Round 10: June 26th

  • Match 1 (13:30 CST) – One Coin vs KingZero
  • Match 2 (17:00 CST) – Pozy vs EDward Gaming
  • Match 3 (20:30 CST) – Titan Esports Club vs My Queen

Round 11: June 27th

  • Match 1 (13:30 CST) – All Gamers vs Leviatan
  • Match 2 (17:00 CST) – 4 AM vs KingZero
  • Match 3 (20:30 CST) – Tyloo vs EDward Gaming

Round 12: June 28th

  • Match 1 (13:30 CST) – One Coin vs My Queen
  • Match 2 (17:00 CST) – Pozy vs Titan Esports Club
  • Match 3 (20:30 CST) – All Gamers vs KingZero

Round 13: July 3rd

  • Match 1 (13:30 CST) – Leviatan vs EDward Gaming
  • Match 2 (17:00 CST) – 4 AM vs My Queen
  • Match 3 (20:30 CST) – Tyloo vs Titan Esports Club

Round 14: July 4th

  • Match 1 (13:30 CST) – One Coin vs Pozy
  • Match 2 (17:00 CST) – All Gamers vs EDward Gaming
  • Match 3 (20:30 CST) – KingZero vs My Queen

Round 15: July 5th

  • Match 1 (13:30 CST) – Tyloo vs One Coin
  • Match 2 (17:00 CST) – Leviatan vs Titan Esports Club
  • Match 3 (20:30 CST) – 4AM vs Pozy

CNL Prize Pool Distribution:

  • 1st : CN¥ 200,000.00 / 420 SI points
  • 2nd : CN¥ 130,000.00 / 300 SI points
  • 3rd : CN¥ 110,000.00 / 250 SI points
  • 4th : CN¥ 95,000.00 / 200 SI points
  • 5th : CN¥ 90,000.00 / 175 SI points
  • 6th : CN¥ 85,000.00 / 140 SI points
  • 7th : CN¥ 80,000.00 / 100 SI points
  • 8th : CN¥ 75,000.00 / 70 SI points
  • 9th : CN¥ 70,000.00 / 35 SI points
  • 10th : CN¥ 65,000.00 / 0 SI points

How Does Stage 1 Set Up for the Esports World Cup?

This is a super simple concept. At the end of Stage 1, every region will undergo a finals round, pushing through a double-elimination bracket to make it to the grand final. Either the winner or the finalists for each region will qualify for the Esports World Cup, slated to land in Paris from July onwards.

Here’s how it works for each region:

APL Oceania

The two participating teams at the APL Oceania Grand Final will qualify for EWC 2026 in Paris.

APL Asia

The winner of the APL Asia Stage 1 Grand Final will qualify for EWC 2026 in Paris.

APL North

The winner of the APL North Stage 1 Grand Final will qualify for EWC 2026 in Paris.

NAL

The top 4 teams in the Stage Finals will qualify for EWC in Paris.

SAL

The top 4 teams in the Stage Finals will qualify for EWC in Paris.

EML

The top 4 teams in the Stage Finals will qualify for EWC in Paris. Team Secret is directly invited to EWC thanks to their win last year.

CNL

The top 4 teams in the Stage Finals will qualify for EWC in Paris.

How to Watch Rainbow Six Siege Esports

If you want to tune into the Rainbow Six Stage 1 action as it unfolds, make sure to follow the R6 Esports Twitch channel.

Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord server if you’re watching this year’s Esports World Cup.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that League’s Bin has been fined heavily by Riot Games

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

item.title: NYT Connections Hints Today (#1090)—Answers for Friday, June 5
item.content:encoded:
It’s time to add another notch onto that NYT Connections streak. In this guide, you’ll find the NYT Connections hints and solutions you need to power through the puzzle and come out the other side without too much fuss. If you’re stuck on today’s NYT Connections challenge, simply refer to the hints below!

Scroll down to get the NYT Connections answers you need!

How to Play NYT Connections

In June 2023, NYT Connections emerged as yet another game in the ever-growing lineup of the New York Times titles. This title soared immediately, and it’s easy to see why. As a free-to-play, accessible New York Times game, NYT Connections is available worldwide and can be played on desktop devices or in a mobile app.

But how do you play NYT Connections?

The NYT team will provide you with a grid of 16 words once a day. The only task left up to you is to sort those words into four groups of four by finding their commonalities. Every NYT Connections puzzle has just one solution, and some words can have double meanings, so it can sometimes be tricky to figure out the answers.

It’s also important to stress that the words are color-coded: yellow, blue, green, and purple. That’s in order of difficulty, from least to most.

What’s critical here is that you get four guesses every day, and if you use those, your NYT Connections answers for today will be out of reach, as it’ll be game over.

Today’s NYT Connections Hints (June 5)

Here are the words for today’s NYT Connections:

  • FLAKE
  • OSCAR
  • WITCH
  • GHOST
  • INCUBUS
  • BREADCRUMB
  • CLUSTER
  • SITUATIONSHIP
  • STRIPTEASE
  • PUFF
  • QUATRAIN
  • FOREST
  • OVEN
  • DISCLOSURE
  • LOOP
  • THE SUBSTANCE

And here are the hints I can offer for today’s NYT Connections:

  • Yellow – Terms you’d associate with the tales of Hansel and Gretel
  • Green – Pieces of cereal
  • Blue – Movies that star Demi Moore
  • Purple – Random words that end in modes of transportation

Here’s the final warning before we get to the NYT Connections answers for today, June 5!

Are you sure you want to scroll further?

Very well!

NYT Connections Answers Explained for Today, June 5 (#1090)

If you want to jump straight to the answers for today’s NYT Connections puzzle, I’ve got you covered:

  • Yellow – BREADCRUMB, FOREST, OVEN, WITCH
  • Green – CLUSTER, FLAKE, LOOP, PUFF
  • Blue – DISCLOSURE, GHOST, STRIPTEASE, THE SUBSTANCE
  • Purple – INCUBUS, OSCAR, QUATRAIN, SITUATIONSHIP

When Does NYT Connections Reset?

That’s today’s puzzle, but if you want the next one, the game resets daily at midnight local time (that’s your local time). You’ll have 24 hours to complete the puzzle when midnight ticks over in your timezone.

How did you do? Did you get a perfect run? Let me know what your streak looks like on the Insider Gaming Discord server

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/nyt-connections-hints-solutions-june-5-1090/

item.title: New Hot Wheels Game ‘Infinite Rush’ Release Date and Trailer Revealed
item.content:encoded:
A reliable insider has informed players about a new Hot Wheels game, Infinite Rush, providing a release date, a trailer, and more details.

It has been quite some time since players received a new Hot Wheels title. The last one was Unleashed 2: Turbocharged, and it was released back in 2023. Three years later, players are awaiting a brand new installment, and it seems like they will get a new Hot Wheels game in 2026.

Hot Wheels Infinite Rush Releases in September 2026

A new report from Dealabs reveals the release date, pricing, platforms, and more for Hot Wheels Infinite Rush. The game is being developed by Milestone, the team responsible for the previous Unleashed installment. The report reveals that Hot Wheels Infinite Rush will be released on September 24, 2026, for PC, PS5, Xbox Series X/S, and the Switch 2. The price has been set at $49.99. The announcement date hasn’t been confirmed, but there’s a chance it will be announced at Summer Game Fest later tonight.

Hot Wheels Infinite Rush Trailer Posted Early

Additionally, a post by billbil-kun on X (formerly Twitter) points to a trailer for Hot Wheels Infinite Rush, which appears to have been accidentally released early by the EB Games Australia Trailers account on YouTube. The one-and-a-half-minute trailer showcases players navigating a new open world for their joyrides. It features in-engine footage that provides a glimpse of the gameplay players can expect. The trailer features a variety of cars, tracks, and environments, along with a massive figure reminiscent of King Kong. Interested readers can check out the new trailer embedded below:

In other news, the release date for Star Wars Zero Company has also surfaced ahead of the official announcement. Additionally, it seems that, with GTA 6 coming out in November, all developers are planning to release their titles in September. What are your thoughts on the Hot Wheels Infinite Rush release date and trailer surfacing online? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Stuntman Hollywood not being allowed to show Jurassic Park in the reveal trailer. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/new-hot-wheels-game-infinite-rush-release-date-and-trailer-revealed/

item.title: Stuntman: Hollywood Wasn’t Allowed to Show Jurassic Park in Reveal Trailer
item.content:encoded:
Stuntman: Hollywood also has Jurassic Park as one of its collaborations, but the developers weren’t allowed to showcase it in the reveal trailer.

During the most recent PlayStation State of Play event, players learned about a new Stuntman game, nearly two decades after the last installment. The trailer featured high-intensity stunts reminiscent of various film properties. However, one notable franchise was absent from the reveal trailer, and we now have some insight into why.

Stuntman: Hollywood Includes Jurassic Park, But It Couldn’t Be Shown in the Reveal Trailer

A new report by Stephen Totilo on GameFile reveals that Stuntman: Hollywood also includes Jurassic Park, but wasn’t allowed to showcase it in the State of Play announcement. Saber CEO Matt Karch disclosed that Saber, the developers of the upcoming Stuntman title, were not permitted to showcase their collaboration. The issue stemmed from a lack of communication over several months regarding Jurassic Park announcements, which also affected the timeline for Stuntman Hollywood’s announcement.

Matt Karch has informed players that he was not given an explanation for the communication issues, but Universal has stated that the problem has now been resolved. The lack of communication affected not only Stuntman Hollywood but also all social media channels associated with the upcoming film, which fell silent from mid-March to June 2. However, it appears that Jurassic Park is back in action on all fronts, with the development of Jurassic Park Survival looking very promising.

Talking about the properties Stuntman Hollywood has, replay iconic moments from Universal Pictures films and NBCUniversal TV shows like Fast & Furious, Back to the Future, Knight Rider, Miami Vice, and Death Race, as well as Jurassic Park, now confirmed through the report.

In other news, check out all the announcements that arrived during the latest PlayStation State of Play event. Additionally, a new God of War game was finally revealed, featuring Faye as the lead. What are your thoughts on Stuntman Hollywood not being allowed to show Jurassic Park? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Madden NFL 27 performance mode delivering over 100 FPS on PS5 Pro. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.
